folio.label: 160r
folio.l: His sadel was of rewel bon
His brydel as the Sonne shon
Or as the Mone ligút
His spere was of fyn Cypres
That bodetú werre & no thy~gö pes
The hed ful sharp I g¼unde
His steede was al appel gray
It gotú an aumble in the wey
Ful softly & rounde
In londe
Loo lordes myn heere is a fyt
If ©e wyln any more of it
To telle it I wol fonde
¢ Now holde ©oure moutú ¹ charite
Bothe knygút and lady free
And herkenetú to my spelle
Of bataille and of cheualry
And of ladies loue drury
A noon I wol ©ow telle
Men speken of romaunces of pés
Of horn~ Child and of Ypotis
Of Beus and Sir Guy
Of Sire lybeus and pleyn damour
But Sire Thopas he beretú the flour
Of real Chyualrie
His good steede , al he bystrod
And fortú vp on his weye he woldrodwoldrodwold Does not have any sigh attempt to cancel it. ODR
As sparcle out of the brond
Vp on his creste he bar a Tour
And æäe Inne stiked a lilye flour
God shilde his cors fro shonde
And fortú he was knygút Auntrous
He nolde slepen in noon hous
But lyggen in his hode
His brigút helm was his wonger
And by hi~ baitetú his destrer
Of Erbes fyne & goode
Hiö self drank water of the welle
As dede the knygút Sire îcyuelle
So worthy vnder wede ;
Til on a day ;
